key_insight: 3M Command strips work by using a highly stretchable adhesive layer that carries high load during service but releases at low force when pulled, with the load-to-release ratio scaling strongly with bonded length over adhesive thickness.
engineering_relevance: The fracture-mechanics framework provides a predictive way to design removable adhesives that maximize holding strength while enabling clean, damage-free removal.
method: Derived finite-deformation analytical expressions for interfacial crack energy release rates.
Compared load-bearing and release conditions for the Command strip geometry.
Validated theory with finite-element J-integral simulations and studied alternating crack paths between interfaces.
evidence_signal: The analytical energy release rates were validated against finite-element J-integral evaluations, and parametric studies produced failure envelopes for competing interface toughnesses.
watch_item: Predicted release behavior depends on accurate interfacial fracture toughness and geometry, so real materials or surface conditions may shift the removal force and crack path.
paper_title: How do 3M Command strips work? A fracture mechanics approach
